criteria_results.criterion: Step detail panel has 'Assigned Tools' section below 'Assigned Roles' section
criteria_results.evidence: Static: step-detail-panel.tsx line 395 renders '/* Assigned Tools */' section. Wrench icon imported from lucide-react (line 4). Section appears after Assigned Roles section (Roles section ends ~line 393 before Separator + Assigned Tools at 395). Label text 'Assigned Tools' confirmed at line 399.

criteria_results.criterion: Dropdown selector lists all workspace tools (fetched via fetchTools), excluding already-assigned tools
criteria_results.evidence: Static: assignedToolIds Set built from stepTools (line 148). DropdownMenuItem has disabled={isAssigned} and onSelect guards with if (!isAssigned) (lines 441-442). allTools populated via fetchTools(workspaceId) per builder notes. Already-assigned tools show checkmark '✓' and are disabled.

criteria_results.criterion: Assigned tools shown as badges with X remove button (matching role assignment pattern exactly)
criteria_results.evidence: Static: lines 403-420. Badge variant='outline' with X button using handleRemoveTool. aria-label on remove button: 'Remove {st.tool.name} tool'. Pattern matches role badge structure.

criteria_results.criterion: Clicking add assigns tool via createStepTool, clicking X removes via deleteStepTool
criteria_results.evidence: Static: createStepTool imported (line 47), called in handleAssignTool at line 170. deleteStepTool imported (line 48), called in handleRemoveTool at line 179. State updated immediately via setStepTools after each operation.

criteria_results.criterion: Tool badges show tool name and optionally cost_per_month
criteria_results.evidence: Static: lines 405-411. Badge shows st.tool.name. Conditional block renders '· $X/mo' when st.tool.cost_per_month != null.

criteria_results.criterion: Cost computation section shows tool cost contribution: sum of assigned tools' cost_per_month
criteria_results.evidence: Static: line 494. const toolMonthlyCost = stepTools.reduce((sum, st) => sum + (st.tool.cost_per_month ?? 0), 0). hasToolCost = toolMonthlyCost > 0 (line 495). Cost block renders if !hasTimeFreq && !hasToolCost is false (line 497), meaning tool cost alone triggers the block.

criteria_results.criterion: Total step monthly cost = labor cost + tool cost (displayed separately)
criteria_results.evidence: Static: line 505. const totalCost = (laborCost ?? 0) + toolMonthlyCost. Labor cost and tool cost shown as separate line items. Total row renders when both exist.
BUILD_RESULT_1.json: typecheck status=pass, errors=0, output='npx tsc --noEmit — no output (clean)'.

criteria_results.criterion: Status dropdown uses local state instead of tool.status prop directly
criteria_results.evidence: Static: tool-detail-panel.tsx line 48: const [status, setStatus] = React.useState(tool.status). Line 186: select onChange uses handleStatusChange. Select value is 'status' local state (not tool.status prop directly).

criteria_results.criterion: Local state resets when tool.id changes (useEffect pattern matching name/category/vendor/url/costPerMonth)
criteria_results.evidence: Static: line 67: setStatus(tool.status) inside useEffect. Line 72: deps array includes tool.id, tool.name, tool.status, tool.category, tool.vendor, tool.url, tool.cost_per_month — matches existing pattern.

criteria_results.criterion: Dropdown change updates local state immediately (optimistic)
criteria_results.evidence: Static: handleStatusChange (line 94): setStatus(newStatus) called immediately before await updateTool call (line 96). Optimistic update confirmed.

criteria_results.criterion: On API error, reverts local state to previous value and shows toast error
criteria_results.evidence: Static: lines 94-101. previous value saved before optimistic update. catch block calls setStatus(previous) to revert. Builder notes confirm toast error shown on revert.

criteria_results.criterion: Tool section detail panel shows 'N tools' count in header area or below name field
criteria_results.evidence: Static: tool-section-detail-panel.tsx line 101: '{toolCount} {toolCount === 1 ? "tool" : "tools"} in this section'. Browser: bodyHasPanel=true — DOM contains tool-section node markup. Page loaded without console errors (errors: []) or network errors (netErrors: []).

criteria_results.criterion: Count is passed as optional toolCount prop from tools-canvas-view.tsx
criteria_results.evidence: Static: tool-section-detail-panel.tsx line 34: toolCount?: number. tools-canvas-view.tsx line 489: toolCount={selectedSectionToolCount}.

criteria_results.criterion: tools-canvas-view.tsx computes count from tools array filtered by tool_section_id
criteria_results.evidence: Static: tools-canvas-view.tsx lines 377-379: selectedSectionToolCount computed via Object.values(toolSectionMap).filter(sId => sId === selectedSectionId).length. Uses spatial toolSectionMap since Tool type has no tool_section_id field (per builder notes).

criteria_results.criterion: Count updates when tools are added/removed from the section
criteria_results.evidence: Static: selectedSectionToolCount is derived from toolSectionMap state, which is updated on drag operations. Adding/removing tools from a section updates the map and triggers recomputation.
BUILD_RESULT_3.json: typecheck status=pass, errors=0. Note: BUILD_RESULT_3 has files_changed=[] as the feature was already implemented in the worktree baseline.

improvements_found.observation: Assigned Tools section in step detail panel shows 'No tools — create tools first' as dropdown label when workspace has no tools. This empty-state microcopy is helpful but a direct link to the Tools page would reduce friction.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H6: Recognition rather than recall — user shouldn't need to remember where to create tools
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Replace static label with a link: 'No tools yet — go to Tools to create some' with a navigation action.

improvements_found.observation: Single-clicking a tool section node does not open the ToolSectionDetailPanel in the accessibility tree (panel may render off-screen or panel open interaction requires different click target). Browser accessibility snapshot showed no panel after clicking section node.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status — user needs clear feedback that clicking selected the section and opened the panel
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Verify panel opens on single click and is visible in viewport; if double-click required, add tooltip 'Double-click to edit'.
